steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a similarity between the two programs — what they share. A correct answer must mention both programs and identify a common feature, not contrast them or focus on only one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'While Flámsbana: Minutt for Minutt depicts a train ride from Myrdal station down to Flám, Hihna 247 depicts a supermarket checkout aisle of the Prisma Kaari grocery store.': The word "while" signals contrast, and the sentence highlights what is different about each program's content — the opposite of emphasizing similarity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Documenting ordinary events in real time, Flámsbana: Minutt for Minutt and Hihna 247 are both documentary programs in the slow TV genre.': This names both programs, states what they do in common (document ordinary events in real time), and explicitly identifies them as belonging to the same genre. It directly emphasizes their shared nature. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Compared to the 12-hour-long Hihna 247, the slow TV program Flámsbana: Minutt for Minutt is relatively short, at 1 hour.': This contrasts the two programs by running time — a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — 'A prime example of the slow TV genre can be seen in Flámsbana: Minutt for Minutt, a 1-hour Norwegian TV documentary program documenting a train ride from Myrdal station down to Flám.': This describes only one program and never mentions Hihna 247, so it cannot establish a similarity between the two. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
